exam_format: Paper-based; two sections: multiple choice and free response. Administered at school in May. AP Studio Art/Drawing: portfolio submission. AP Research/Seminar: academic paper and oral presentation.
exam_price_notes: $98 per exam; $55 for students with fee reductions; +$40 international surcharge

testing_centers.notes: AP exams are administered at school sites in May. Some international testing available at authorized international centers. Students must be enrolled in the AP course or register independently through their school.

id_requirements: School ID or government-issued photo ID required. Name on registration must match ID exactly.
exam_rules: No electronic devices (including phones and smartwatches) permitted during the exam. Approved calculators allowed for some sections (e.g., AP Calculus, AP Statistics, AP Physics). AP Studio Art and AP Research/Seminar are assessed via portfolio or academic paper rather than a timed written exam. All exams are paper-based (multiple choice + free-response) except AP Computer Science Principles (some digital components).
renewal_options: AP scores never expire; once earned, scores are valid indefinitely.

retake_policy.notes: AP exams are offered once per year in May. Students may retake the exam in a subsequent year; there is no same-year retake. Scores from all attempts are reported to colleges unless the student requests score cancellation.

official_study_resources.title: AP Students Course and Exam Description
official_study_resources.notes: Free Course and Exam Descriptions (CEDs) available per AP subject at apstudents.collegeboard.org

official_study_resources.title: AP Classroom Practice Questions and AP Daily Videos
official_study_resources.notes: Free via AP Classroom (myAP); access through enrolled school or teacher
